mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as higher financing costs weigh on corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window, or high-rate periods show no significant negative return bias (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as markets price in recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, invalidating the recession-risk transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a low or falling federal funds rate jointly signal robust consumer demand financed by accommodative credit conditions, a combination that historically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because both consumption strength and cheap financing support earnings growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The joint-condition subsample shows no statistically significant return premium over the unconditional 20d return distribution (bootstrap p > 0.10), or the RSAFS/FEDFUNDS interaction term has lower explanatory power than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate housing expansion regime, rising housing starts signal durable-goods and construction-linked demand that feeds through to broader corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this effect is expected only when the rate backdrop remains supportive of housing credit.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month trend positive while FEDFUNDS below 3%
failure_condition: During periods meeting the regime condition, forward 60d returns show no significant positive skew relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the relationship reverses sign when FEDFUNDS rises above 3% within the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that triggers rapid negative repricing of cyclical equities within the following trading week as markets react to unexpected weakness in manufacturing output.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO drops are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) across a rolling 3-year sample, or the effect disappears after excluding recession-dated observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects ongoing labor market expansion and income growth that supports near-term consumer spending, producing modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as a continuous, always-active signal of economic momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation accelerates beyond a 3% year-over-year threshold, markets anticipate tighter forthcoming monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors discount future rate hikes and margin compression.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPI YoY > 3% are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or the signal fails to outperform a naive baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a simultaneous rise in unemployment alongside still-elevated federal funds rates signals that policy has begun to bite the labor market, creating expectations of an imminent policy pivot that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price in future easing ahead of realized data.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising 3-month trend AND FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: In the joint-trigger subsample, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the pivot-anticipation effect is indistinguishable from cases where FEDFUNDS remains below 4% with rising unemployment.
